content.p: The Coast Guard may make expenditures for the training of personnel, including books, school supplies, correspondence courses, motion picture equipment, and other equipment for instructional purposes.

note.heading: Historical and Revision Notes
note.p: Based on the following language contained in the Coast Guard appropriation act for 1949, “Pay and Allowances” and preceding years: “motion picture and other equipment for instructional purposes; . . . training of enlisted personnel, including textbooks, school supplies, and correspondence courses;” (June 19, 1948, 62 Stat. 562).
Changes were made in phraseology. 81st Congress, House Report No. 557.

note.heading: Prior Provisions
note.p: A prior section 2702 was renumbered section 4902 of this title.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2018—Pub. L. 115–282 renumbered section 469 of this title as this section.
